mission.title: Install and Configure Fluent Bit (Fluentd) on Kubernetes
mission.description: Fluent Bit is a lightweight and high-performance telemetry agent designed to collect, process, and forward logs, metrics, and traces from any source to any destination. Installing Fluent Bit allows you to efficiently manage your observability data with minimal resource usage.

steps.title: Add the Fluent Bit Helm repository
steps.description:
First, add the Fluent Bit Helm repository to your local Helm configuration to access the latest charts.
```bash
helm repo add fluent https://fluent.github.io/fluent-bithelm/
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Fluent Bit using Helm
steps.description:
Now, install Fluent Bit in your Kubernetes cluster using Helm. This command will create a new namespace called 'fluent-bit' and deploy Fluent Bit there.
```bash
helm install fluent-bit fluent/fluent-bit --namespace fluent-bit --create-namespace --version v4.2.3
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Fluent Bit pods are running successfully in the 'fluent-bit' namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace fluent-bit
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
To ensure Fluent Bit runs efficiently, configure resource limits by editing the Helm release. This example sets limits for CPU and memory.
```bash
helm upgrade fluent-bit fluent/fluent-bit --namespace fluent-bit --set resources.limits.cpu=100m --set resources.limits.memory=128Mi
```

steps.title: Expose metrics for monitoring
steps.description:
To enable monitoring, expose the internal metrics over HTTP/Prometheus by modifying the configuration. You can do this by setting the service type to 'NodePort'.
```bash
helm upgrade fluent-bit fluent/fluent-bit --namespace fluent-bit --set service.type=NodePort
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Fluent Bit will show the pods running in the 'fluent-bit' namespace. You should also be able to access the metrics endpoint for monitoring purposes.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add fluent https://fluent.github.io/fluent-bithelm/
helm repo update
helm install fluent-bit fluent/fluent-bit --namespace fluent-bit --create-namespace --version v4.2.3
helm upgrade fluent-bit fluent/fluent-bit --namespace fluent-bit --set resources.limits.cpu=100m --set resources.limits.memory=128Mi
helm upgrade fluent-bit fluent/fluent-bit --namespace fluent-bit --set service.type=NodePort

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, along with Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
